The **2014 Brisbane Broncos season** was the 27th in [the club](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos")'s history. Coached by [Anthony Griffin](/wiki/Anthony_Griffin_(rugby_league) "Anthony Griffin (rugby league)") and co-captained by [Justin Hodges](/wiki/Justin_Hodges "Justin Hodges") and [Corey Parker](/wiki/Corey_Parker_(rugby_league) "Corey Parker (rugby league)"), they competed in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League")'s [2014 Telstra Premiership](/wiki/2014_NRL_season "2014 NRL season"), finishing the regular season eighth (out of sixteen) to make it into the finals. The Broncos were then knocked out of contention in the first game of the finals against the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").

## Season summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2014_Brisbane_Broncos_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Season summary")]

On 10 January, [Sam Thaiday](/wiki/Sam_Thaiday "Sam Thaiday") announced he was standing down from the Broncos captaincy.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Thaiday had captained the Broncos since 2012 when he took over the captaincy from the retired [Darren Lockyer](/wiki/Darren_Lockyer "Darren Lockyer"). [Justin Hodges](/wiki/Justin_Hodges "Justin Hodges") and [Corey Parker](/wiki/Corey_Parker_(rugby_league) "Corey Parker (rugby league)") were named the club's new co-captains.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On 21 July, Coach [Anthony Griffin](/wiki/Anthony_Griffin_(rugby_league) "Anthony Griffin (rugby league)") was informed his services would not be required in 2015.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Griffin agreed to see out the remainder of the 2014 season. When asked by the media what he said to the players, Griffin said "I told them I got the arse". At the time of his sacking Griffin had a coaching record of 51 wins from 93 games.

Later the same day, it was announced that [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)") had signed a three-year deal to coach the Broncos from 2015.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Bennett is the inaugural coach of the Broncos having coached them from 1988 to 2008.

#### NRL Auckland Nines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2014_Brisbane_Broncos_season&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: NRL Auckland Nines")]

Main article: [2014 NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/2014_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2014 NRL Auckland Nines")

The [NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/NRL_Auckland_Nines "NRL Auckland Nines") is a pre-season [rugby league nines](/wiki/Rugby_league_nines "Rugby league nines") competition featuring all 16 [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") clubs. The 2014 competition was played over two days on 15 and 16 February at [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park") in Auckland, New Zealand. The Broncos featured in Pool Green and played the [Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs"), [Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels") and [Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters"). The top two teams of each pool qualified for the quarter-finals.
